evals.description: Audit a repo with OAuth/token auth code
evals.setup: Repository with OAuth refresh token implementation and API key usage
evals.expected_output: Should produce a full 6-category report with COVERED/PARTIAL/MISSING per vector
expectations: Searches for OAuth-related code patterns (refresh tokens, grants, state parameter)
Checks token invalidation on account disable/password change
Reports token logging exposure risk
Classifies each item with evidence (file:line citations for COVERED)
Generates remediation list ordered by severity

evals.description: Audit Hydra CI/CD pipeline with hydra argument
evals.setup: Hydra repository with pipeline scripts and persona containers
evals.expected_output: Should load Hydra-specific reference and check all H1-H7 vectors in addition to generic checklist
expectations: Loads references/hydra-persistence-vectors.md
Checks per-persona PAT scoping (builder vs reviewer vs security)
Audits yolo auto-merge trust chain
Checks label state machine integrity and reconciliation window
Checks log integrity and token masking in pipeline logs
Reports on container isolation boundaries

evals.setup: Repository where credentials were recently rotated after a suspected breach
evals.expected_output: Should treat PARTIAL as effectively MISSING in severity scoring
expectations: Uses heightened severity: PARTIAL items appear in critical gaps list
Checks whether credential rotation actually revoked all trust paths
Verifies audit trail integrity (could evidence have been tampered with?)
Recommends re-audit after remediation
Suggests verifying COVERED items are enforced, not just documented

evals.description: Audit OpenSpec design documents instead of code
evals.setup: Repository with openspec/ directory containing architecture specs and design docs
evals.expected_output: Should audit spec documents for persistence coverage and flag gaps in the design
expectations: Reads openspec/ specs and architecture documents
Identifies which persistence vectors are addressed in the design
Flags vectors not mentioned in specs as MISSING
Recommends code-level follow-up audit

evals.description: Audit a system with minimal attack surface
evals.prompt: Run /persistence-audit on this static site repo
evals.setup: Simple static site with no auth, no CI/CD secrets, no service accounts
evals.expected_output: Should correctly mark most categories N/A with justification
expectations: Does not fabricate findings for non-existent attack surfaces
Marks OAuth, service accounts, IdP as N/A with clear reasoning
Still checks CI/CD (GitHub Actions may have secrets) and endpoint persistence
Completes all 6 categories — does not skip any

should_trigger: can an attacker maintain access after key rotation
audit this repo for persistence risks
check if credential rotation is sufficient
run a persistence audit on hydra
did we eradicate or just contain
audit CI/CD pipeline for secret persistence
check if an attacker could survive credential rotation
